steps.title: Spot the two independent clauses
steps.content: The first independent clause describes the temperature at site CC1, with an appositive phrase: “the air temperature was 17.9°C at site CC1, the site with the highest ______.” The second independent clause is “it had shifted to 16.2°C by 8:30 p.m.”

steps.title: Apply the comma-plus-conjunction rule
steps.content:
A comma plus a coordinating conjunction correctly joins two independent clauses; choice B provides “elevation, and,” which both completes the first clause and supplies the required punctuation and conjunction.

Choices A, C, and D violate this rule by each omitting a critical element (A: no punctuation or conjunction; C: no comma; D: no conjunction).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
